taskDescription: Arrange the steps of examining a patient with suspected knee injury in correct clinical sequence.
paragraphs: Inspect the knee for swelling (haemarthrosis) and deformity, and note the position of the limb.
Palpate the joint line, medial and lateral collateral ligaments, and patella for tenderness.
Test the medial collateral ligament by applying valgus stress with the knee slightly flexed.
Perform the anterior drawer test and Lachman test to assess the anterior cruciate ligament integrity.
Check for meniscal injury using McMurray's test — flexion, rotation, and extension of the knee.
Assess the popliteal fossa for Baker's cyst or popliteal artery aneurysm.
